Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Is it neccessary to read any other book aside from Bhagavad Gita?

Sadhana · asked by user [] · 2008-03-23 · 2 answers
"Because Bhagavad-guc0u299 tu257 is spoken by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, one need not read any other Vedic literature. One need only attentively and regularly hear and read Bhagavad-gu299 tu257 . In the present age, people are so absorbed in mundane activities that it is not possible for them to read all the Vedic literatures. But this is not necessary. This one book, Bhagavad-gu299 tu257 , will suffice, because it is the essence of all Vedic literatures and especially because it is spoken by the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
Gu299 tu257 -mu257 hu257 tmya 4
user [170] · 2008-03-25
I never read anything bar the first two chapters of Bhagavad Gita. I still can not understand it and do not want to go any further untill I understand.
user [38] · 2008-03-25
Try study guides from our site: http://www.veda.harekrsna.cz/library/Bhagavad-gita.zip
so that you wont talk nonsense about suicidal gurus (if that was a joke then it was a really bad one).
